competitive_position: Conga (Salesforce CLM) is the single strongest threat — the EB-sponsored incumbent the RFP exists to test, positioned as lower-risk 'enhance vs replace.' Zycus wins on external-legal Word redlining, clause library, risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin AI. Coupa is dormant; DocuSign/Certinal appear only as comparison-case-study asks. Do-nothing/enhance-Conga is the active fallback to beat on differentiation and price.

champion.summary: Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion credited with getting the RFP launched, but he lacks power-line access to skeptical EB Bevan and admits no prior Zycus experience — making him the EB-broker is the highest-leverage use of his strength.

items.quote: legal is external, so we redline with them as an external partner
the complexity behind that and integrating with our systems... is your concern around privacy and security
how much does it cost for this module and that module... how many times do I get it nickeled and dimed
Art, I think, is very skeptical still

recommended_moves: Win the proposal on differentiation + price: build the RFP response around the exact Conga gaps ARUP codified (external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, D365/Merlin) AND embed a bundled TCO/ROI model that answers the RFP pricing questions (power vs business users, e-sign envelope volume, supplier counts).

Pre-stage paper and InfoSec in parallel with the proposal: name a Zycus paper owner, send the standard MSA/SOW/Order Form and a SOC2/security packet to Mzia/Michael for routing to external counsel, and confirm the InfoSec process.

Arm Michael to broker the leadership conversation: equip him with the differentiation-led Conga comparison, ROI narrative, and Gartner positioning to take to Bevan and the risk/legal exec ('Jonathan') so the value case reaches power through the champion who authored the RFP.

IN the meantime, I sent Gartner Report + Information on Procurecon west to support their internal discussions with leadership. Aligned for March timeline

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: EB Mike Bevan controls the decision and budget but has never been on a Zycus call. Post-demo he 'did not see anything... that proved Zycus could do anything we weren't currently able to do' (03 Sep 2025) and mandated the Conga gap analysis. In this champion-authored tender the path to Bevan runs THROUGH Michael, not around him — arming the champion with a leadership-facing value narrative is the move, not cold EB outreach.
